summary: Completed comprehensive frontend testing of The Basic Ecosystem marketing site. All 22 test cases passed: hero, sticky nav with smooth scroll, all sections (HowItWorks, WhoWeWorkWith, WhyChooseUs, Results), consultation form (empty submit blocked, missing property type shows sonner error toast, valid submit shows success state, reset returns to form), final CTA, footer w/ social icons, and mobile menu toggle.

issues: Mobile menu panel (bg #F2F6FB) renders only under the header strip, but at top of page when nav is unscrolled the header itself is transparent — when menu is open the close icon area still sits on the transparent header so the hero text faintly bleeds behind the upper portion of the panel. Minor polish: consider applying solid bg to the entire header when mobile menu is open.

critical_code_review_comments: Consultation form: name/email Input components use HTML5 `required` attribute, but Property Type (Radix Select) is not natively required — current validation is correctly handled in handleSubmit. Good.

Navigation mobile-nav-link testid for 'Why Us' becomes 'mobile-nav-link-why us' (with space) due to `.toLowerCase()` without replacing whitespace — while desktop uses '/\s/g' replace to produce 'why-us'. Consider aligning mobile testids to dashes (e.g., `mobile-nav-link-why-us`) for consistency.
App.js uses BrowserRouter with only a single '/' route — fine for SPA marketing site but Routes wrapper is unnecessary overhead; acceptable for future expansion.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Marketing single-page site, fully client-side. All data-testids match the spec. Consultation form submission is local-state only (no backend). Sonner toasts appear bottom-right with [data-sonner-toast] elements. Property Type uses Radix Select — click [data-testid='form-property-type'] then click div[role='option']:has-text('Villa'). Smooth scroll completes within ~800-1200ms; tests use page.wait_for_timeout(1200) after nav clicks. Mobile menu testid for 'Why Us' is 'mobile-nav-link-why us' (with space, not dash) — desktop equivalent is 'nav-link-why-us'.
